meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Portfolio Building for Students: Escape the 'Basic App' Rut
content.seo_description: Break out of tutorial hell. Learn how to transform basic projects into a professional portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert guide.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are building tutorials, not products. To level up, shift from following instructions to solving specific, real-world problems. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can ship a high-impact portfolio project that proves your engineering capability to potential employers.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks following YouTube tutorials, churning out yet another To-Do list app or a generic weather dashboard. It feels like progress, but deep down, you know the truth: you’re stuck in tutorial hell. Your code is a carbon copy of someone else's thinking, and you have nothing to show that proves you can architect a system from scratch.

The industry doesn't care about your ability to follow a video; it cares about your ability to solve problems. It is time to stop building apps and start building software. By integrating Antigravity’s rapid-prototyping architecture with Claude Code’s iterative intelligence, we will move your work from 'student exercise' to 'professional showcase' in record time.

steps.title: Define a Real-World Problem
steps.description: Stop building generic apps. Identify a manual process in your daily life or a local business that needs automation. Your portfolio needs a story, not just features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Architect. Help me define a scope for a SaaS tool that solves [Insert Problem]. Focus on a core MVP feature set that can be built in under 10 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Propose a technical stack for a MVP that solves [Problem] focusing on scalability and clean architecture.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, map the data relationships. Use Antigravity to generate a schema that reflects enterprise-grade patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema and API structure for [App Name]. Use relational best practices, include indexing strategies, and ensure the schema is highly normalized.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the migration files and ORM models based on the schema generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Rapid Scaffolding
steps.description: Stop manually boilerplate-ing. Use Antigravity to instantiate your core infrastructure, ensuring you spend your time on business logic rather than configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the project boilerplate using the previously defined schema. Include authentication, error handling, and structured logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project repository and set up the CI/CD pipeline for deployment.'

steps.title: Iterative Development with Claude
steps.description: Implement features one by one. Use Claude Code to write the logic, then perform a 'code review' on your own work to improve performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the following business logic for [Feature] to be more modular, readable, and performant according to clean code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'Implement [Feature] logic with comprehensive unit tests for edge cases.'

steps.title: Professional Documentation
steps.description: A project is only as good as its documentation. Create a README that explains the 'Why' behind your architecture, not just the 'How'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for my project. Include a system architecture diagram description, setup instructions, and a 'Why I chose this tech stack' section.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate technical documentation and add it to the docs folder.'

pro_tips: Always build for the 'edge case'—employers look for how you handle failures, not just the happy path.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests before you write the implementation code to ensure TDD best practices.
Document your failures in your README; showing how you debugged a complex issue is more impressive than a bug-free repo.
Limit your MVP scope aggressively; a polished, small-scale app beats a half-finished 'super app' every time.
Use semantic commit messages to demonstrate professional workflow habits to recruiters viewing your GitHub.
content.next_steps: Deploy your app to a public URL using Vercel or Railway, then write a short blog post on LinkedIn explaining the problem you solved and the technical challenges you overcame.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the roadmap to stop being a student and start being an engineer. Every line of code you write today is an asset for your future career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
